user_prompt: first, look at `inc\version.h`, implement the strings in `src\version.c` using as much of compiler preprocessor definitions as possible.
`syncToCompileTime` needs to use `compiler_time_str`, if `compiler_time_str` is in the future, sync the physical RTC to it.
somehow make sure `version.c` is compiled every build, maybe something to do with `platformio.ini`, maybe adding a script to `tools`
it is acceptable if `src\version.c` is automatically generated by a tool, as long as `inc\version.h` stays consistent
do not touch other files not mentioned
agent_reply: - Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.
Verification: `pio run` passed. I also ran a second build and confirmed PlatformIO recompiled `src/version.c.o` again, so the pre-build regeneration is doing its job.
- Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.
Verification: `pio run` passed. I also ran a second build and confirmed PlatformIO recompiled `src/version.c.o` again, so the pre-build regeneration is doing its job.

user_prompt: during password reset through the web interface, I get `N.Pbkdf2HmacSha256 is not a function` as the error, something is wrong with the webcrypt shim?
agent_reply: Yep, that was the shim. Its PBKDF2 path tries to call `asmCrypto.Pbkdf2HmacSha256`, but the bundled shim does not actually provide that function, so password reset dies before it can POST the new key.

I fixed [sec_helpers.js](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/sec_helpers.js:33) so `derive_pbkdf2_bytes()` still tries WebCrypto first, but falls back to a local JS PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 implementation if the shim/native path fails. I also rebuilt the packaged web asset in [web_assets.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h:3).
Verified the fallback against Node’s `crypto.pbkdf2Sync` for known vectors, then built:
Both passed. Sec0 had one transient stale `.pio` object failure on the first try, then passed cleanly on retry.

user_prompt: I got one successful upload, and a few that are partial, ending in a error 500, the file exists on the card but much smaller than expected
agent_reply: I changed the upload path to behave like a real upload session instead of reopening/appending/syncing the SD file on every network chunk.

- `AsyncFsManager` now has `openFileForUpload()`, `writeUploadFileChunk()`, `closeUploadFile()`, and upload session IDs in [AsyncFsManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp).

- `/file_upload` now opens once on the first chunk, writes chunks sequentially by offset, verifies the final raw-body byte count, then syncs/closes once at finish in [WebFileHandlers.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp).
- Client disconnect cleanup now only closes the matching upload session, so a late disconnect cannot kill a newer upload.
- Web UI upload timeout went from 2 minutes to 10 minutes in [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js).
This should fix the “partial file then 500” behavior if it was caused by per-chunk SD sync/open/close overhead or browser timeout.
